repo_prior_art.cite: pid_gpn_continuous_rho: fixed-rho continuous GPN inverse-threshold proposal, not Spearman-band Pr(Y1>Y0) endpoints

published_prior_art.cite: FanPark2009DTEConfidenceSets and FanPark2010DTEBounds treat known-dependence or copula-free DTE bounds; BartlKupperLuxPapapantoleon2022Dependence gives non-causal dependence-uncertainty OT duality; none subsumes causal Spearman-band benefit endpoints
General interval-identified decision logic is standard; FirpoRidder2019Functionals motivates direct functional bounds but does not state this Spearman-band majority envelope

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_bound_intersection_inference: failed generic endpoint-inference warning, not this finite-cell Spearman-grid estimator

published_prior_art.cite: FanPark2010DTEBounds gives plug-in endpoint inference for Makarov bounds; the finite-cell rank-moment transport sieve is a distinct but routine extension

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_bound_intersection_inference: failed generic bootstrap/active-set angle, not stable-dual-face Spearman endpoint law

published_prior_art.cite: ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MoreCI supply interval-CI logic; FanPark2010DTEBounds supplies fewer-than-n bootstrap precedent; endpoint law for Spearman-band transport remains distinct

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The majority-sign statement is accepted only as a definitional supporting corollary once the endpoint functions are established.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Do not treat Theorem 1 as the headline novelty; the headline remains Conjecture 1.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Estimator consistency is accepted only in the finite-cell regime with the checkerboard bias condition assumed in Assumption 6.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: No continuous-X nuisance-smoothing or uniform inference claim is bound to Theorem 2.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Endpoint inference is conditional on stable dual faces and directional differentiability as stated in Assumption 5.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inference claim is a conditional field-tier endpoint-law conjecture, not an unconditional bootstrap theorem.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add FrandsenLefgren2021KIPP to the Conjecture 1 results-at-a-glance comparator sentence, since it is the closest published nonparametric dependence-restriction DTE bounds paper even though it uses stochastic increasingness rather than a Spearman band.

Add a small two- or three-point quantile-grid exhibit computing one lower and one upper Spearman-band endpoint; this is not required for field-tier acceptance but would make the focal object auditable and reduce downstream SC10 risk if the angle is later promoted.
State an explicit singleton-rho corollary showing how the band endpoint collapses to the known-dependence problem, then use the unrestricted-band sanity check as the opposite boundary case.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 on the field-tier Spearman-band partial-identification kernel, keeping Theorem 1 as supporting material.
